name: Garrucha to Mojácar Old Town Loop
description: Hike the moderate 10.5-mile (16.9 km) Mojacar from Garrucha trail for stunning coastal views and historical landmarks.
region_intro: Hiking around Garrucha offers diverse landscapes, from coastal paths along the Mediterranean Sea to more varied inland terrain. The region features flat coastal promenades connecting to nearby towns and provides access to unique natural areas. These include the volcanic landscapes and cliffs of the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park and the arid scenery of the Tabernas Desert. The area also encompasses wetlands like Salar de los Canos, known for birdwatching, and hilltop villages such as Mojácar Old Town. Best hiking trails around Garrucha The most popular hiking route is Garrucha to Mojácar Old Town Loop, a 10.5 miles (16.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects the coastal town with the historic hilltop village, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Garrucha – Vera Beach loop from Garrucha, an easy 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path. This coastal trail provides scenic views of the Mediterranean Sea and a vibrant seaside atmosphere. Local hikers also love the Iron Foundry Chimney – El Playazo Beach Bar loop from Garrucha, a 7.0 miles (11.3 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 2 hours 52 minutes. Hiking around Garrucha is defined by coastal paths, varied terrain, and proximity to unique natural parks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate routes with elevation gains. The routes in Garrucha are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Garrucha's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Garrucha?
A: There are over 15 hiking trails around Garrucha, offering a variety of experiences. Most routes are classified as easy or moderate, making the area accessible for many hikers.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near Garrucha?
A: The region around Garrucha offers diverse terrain, from picturesque coastal paths along the Mediterranean Sea to more varied inland routes with some elevation gains. You'll find flat promenades, volcanic landscapes, and even arid desert scenery nearby.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in Garrucha?
A: Yes, Garrucha has several easy routes suitable for families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21835720" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garrucha – Vera Beach loop from Garrucha&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.6 km coastal path offering scenic views and a vibrant seaside atmosphere. Another easy choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40378044" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vera Beach – Puerto Rey Lagoon loop from Garrucha&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 4.9 km.

Q: What are some interesting natural attractions to see while hiking near Garrucha?
A: Hikers can explore several natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4282404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salar de los Caños Wetland&lt;/a&gt; near Vera Beach is a great spot for birdwatching. The nearby Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park offers unspoiled volcanic landscapes and dramatic cliffs, while the Tabernas Desert provides a unique arid environment.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Garrucha?
A: Yes, many routes around Garrucha are circular. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983775500/garrucha-to-mojacar-old-town-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garrucha to Mojácar Old Town Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 16.9 km trail connecting the coast with the historic hilltop village. Another popular circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38256039" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Foundry Chimney – El Playazo Beach Bar loop from Garrucha&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.3 km.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Garrucha?
A: The shoulder seasons (spring and autumn) are generally ideal for hiking in Garrucha, offering pleasant temperatures. Winter can also be a good time for hiking, with milder weather. Summers can be very hot, especially inland, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if hiking during this period.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I visit on a hike near Garrucha?
A: You can combine hiking with exploring historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5060861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojácar Old Town&lt;/a&gt;, with its Moorish architecture and spectacular views, is a popular destination. Near Vera, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2831149" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Statue of the Sacred Heart of Jesus on Cerro del Espíritu Santo&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas and historical context.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Garrucha?
A: The hiking routes around Garrucha are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from scenic coastal paths to the unique natural parks, and the variety of routes suitable for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any hikes that offer views of the Tabernas Desert?
A: While the Tabernas Desert itself is a distinct hiking area, some routes or viewpoints around Garrucha and Mojácar Old Town may offer distant views of its unique arid scenery, providing a striking contrast to the coastal landscapes.

Q: Are there opportunities for birdwatching on hikes near Garrucha?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4282404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salar de los Caños Wetland&lt;/a&gt;, located near Vera Beach, is an excellent spot for birdwatching. This natural wetland features two lagoons and is home to over 150 documented bird species, including flamingos, with accessible walking paths and observatories.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes around Garrucha?
A: Hikes around Garrucha vary in length and duration. Easy coastal strolls, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40376925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cargadero loop from Garrucha&lt;/a&gt; (5.7 km), can take around 1.5 hours. Longer, moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983775500/garrucha-to-mojacar-old-town-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garrucha to Mojácar Old Town Loop&lt;/a&gt; (16.9 km), can take up to 4 hours 40 minutes.
tips: Beautiful views of all of Mojácar from the top of Picacho.

The beautiful white Moorish village of Mojácar is situated on a rock, 2 km from the sea, and from afar you have a beautiful view of the typical Andalusian village. The village is part of the coast from Mojácar to San Juan de los Terreros. From Mojácar you have beautiful views over the sea, the Tabernas desert

It corresponds to the coastal defense line built in the 18th century to protect the coast from North African pirates. This one in Garrucha was built in 1769 by order of Carlos III. In the 19th century it was the Carabineros barracks. It has recently been rehabilitated as Aula del Mar.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike from Garrucha to Mojácar?
faqs.answer: The hike from Garrucha to Mojácar is approximately 16.9 kilometers (10.5 miles) long and typically takes around 4 hours and 41 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, offering a mix of paved roads (about 7.13 km), asphalt sections (around 4.36 km), and dedicated paths or streets (approximately 5.40 km and 3.46 km). You'll also encounter narrow, rocky paths carved into a geologically diverse landscape, with gentle ascents and descents parallel to the sea.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: This route blends natural beauty with historical significance. Key highlights include the Panoramic view of Mojacar, the charming Mojácar Old Town, and the historical Las Escobetas Castle (Garrucha Sea Museum). You'll also pass by 18th-century coastal defenses like Torre de Macenas and the older Torre del Pirrulico, and may spot the unique "Piedra del Nazareno" rock formation.

faqs.question: Is the Mojacar from Garrucha trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is generally accessible, specific regulations for dogs can vary along the different sections, especially near developed beachfronts or protected areas. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash and be prepared to clean up after them. Check local signage for any restrictions, particularly on beaches or in town centers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Almerían coastline is known for its exceptional light, making it beautiful year-round. However, for comfortable hiking, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are ideal, offering pleasant temperatures and less intense sun than the summer months. Winters are generally mild, but some facilities might have reduced hours.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike this coastal route between Mojácar and Garrucha. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park if I want to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Parking is available in both Garrucha and Mojácar. In Garrucha, you can often find street parking near the promenade or designated parking areas. In Mojácar Playa, there are various parking options, though it can get busy during peak season. If starting from Mojácar Pueblo, parking is available at the base of the hill or in designated areas within the village.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You will find yourself on parts of Las Huertas de Mojácar, Camino del Puertecico, and Paseo Marítimo during your hike.

faqs.question: Is this a good trail for beginners?
faqs.answer: While classified as moderate due to its length and varied terrain, the trail features easily accessible paths and gentle elevation changes, making it suitable for hikers with a good basic fitness level. Beginners who are prepared for a longer walk should find it manageable.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the trail connects two coastal towns, you'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and bars, especially in the more developed beachfront areas of Mojácar Playa and Garrucha. It's a good idea to carry water, but opportunities for refreshments are available.

faqs.question: What makes the scenery on this hike special?
faqs.answer: The trail offers spectacular coastal views of the stunning Almerían coastline, featuring a mix of developed beachfronts and secluded, 'virgin' beaches. From higher vantage points, you can observe the clear seabed and even distant views of the Tabernas desert. The area's exceptional light has historically attracted artists and filmmakers, adding to its unique charm.
